Notice Of Defendant Housing Authority Of The City And County Of San Francisco?S Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Williams And Plaintiff Watson For Failure To Follow Discovery Orders; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HOUSING AUTHORITY OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY 1815 EGBERT AVE'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Williams And Plaintiff Watson For Failure To Follow Discovery Orders.

Defendant the San Francisco Housing Authority's motion for sanctions is denied without prejudice.

The Housing Authority seeks sanctions for plaintiffs Andre Williams and Timojene Watson's failures to comply with the court's prior discovery orders. Code of Civil Procedure, section 2023.040, states: "A request for a sanction shall, in the notice of motion, identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought, and specify the type of sanction sought. . . ." The Housing Authority's notice of motion states only that it seeks an order "sanctioning" plaintiffs and does not specify the type of sanction sought. The Housing Authority's motion is procedurally deficient, and it is therefore denied without prejudice.

Nothing in this order should be taken to suggest that any party may avoid full compliance with court orders. The court also observes that Plaintiff's opposition contains the following statement: " 'Sanctions should not be imposed where a party has made a good faith effort to comply with discovery obligations.' -Diepenbrock v. Brown (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 743." The quoted language does not appear in Diepenbrock, which reversed a sanctions order because "conflicting legal authority on an unsettled issue provided substantial justification for [the sanctioned party's] position." (Diepenbrock v. Brown (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 743, 749.) The court cautions plaintiffs that it is misleading to the court for them to quote cases that do not contain the quoted language. Self-represented litigants have the same duty not to mislead the court as attorneys. (Kobayashi v. Superior Court (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 536, 543.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Apr-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to state facts showing compliance with the Tort Claims Act, including the date of compliance and whether the complaint was filed pursuant to an express rejection or a rejection by operation of law. Plaintiffs shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. The Court further notes that Plaintiffs appear to have added a claim for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Following an order sustaining a demurrer . . . the plaintiff may amend his or her complaint only as authorized by the court's order. [Citation.] The plaintiff may not amend the complaint to add a new cause of action without having obtained permission to do so. (Harris v. Wachovia Mortgage, FSB (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1018, 1023. Plaintiffs' claim for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress shall be excluded from any further amended complaint unless and until Plaintiffs seek leave.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
